model_1.architecture_text:
def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.fc1 = nn.Linear(17, 128)
        self.fc2 = nn.Linear(128, 64)
        self.fc3 = nn.Linear(64, 32)
        self.drop = nn.Dropout(0.2)
        self.out = nn.Linear(32, 2)  # Binary classification: 0 (No) or 1 (Yes)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = F.relu(self.fc1(x))
        x = self.drop(x)
        x = F.relu(self.fc2(x))
        x = self.drop(x)
        x = F.relu(self.fc3(x))

model_7.architecture_text:
4 widerer layers
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.fc1 = nn.Linear(17, 512)
        self.fc2 = nn.Linear(512, 256)
        self.fc3 = nn.Linear(256, 128)
        self.fc4 = nn.Linear(128, 64)
        self.drop = nn.Dropout(0.2)
        self.out = nn.Linear(64, 2)  # Binary classification: 0 (No) or 1 (Yes)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = F.relu(self.fc1(x))
        x = self.drop(x)
        x = F.relu(self.fc2(x))
        x = self.drop(x)
        x = F.relu(self.fc3(x))
        x = self.drop(x)
        x = F.relu(self.fc4(x))
        return self.out(x)

        super().__init__()

        self.fc2 = nn.Linear(128, 64)
        self.fc3 = nn.Linear(64, 32)
        self.fc4 = nn.Linear(32, 16)
        self.fc5 = nn.Linear(16, 8)

        self.drop = nn.Dropout(0.2)
        self.out = nn.Linear(8, 2)  # Binary classification: 0 (No) or 1 (Yes)

        x = self.drop(x)
        x = F.relu(self.fc2(x))
        x = self.drop(x)
        x = F.relu(self.fc3(x))
        x = self.drop(x)
        x = F.relu(self.fc4(x))
        x = self.drop(x)
        x = F.relu(self.fc5(x))
